Customer or SKU revenue in the period.
Support, delivery rework and account service load.
Returns, credits and rework tied to the account.
Raw material and purchased parts for the quote scope.

Electricity, gas or utility load attributed to the job.
Allocated overhead such as rent, admin and indirect labor.
Expected scrap or waste as a percent of direct cost stack.
Programming, tooling and first-off setup burden.
Freight, packaging and delivery cost for the quote.
Financing or delay cost from customer payment terms.

Mark when entered costs already include sales tax or VAT.
Extra margin points added to build the minimum safe price floor.
Training, process control and prevention activities.
Scrap, rework, warranty and downtime from defects.
Cost of goods sold over twelve months.

Expected markdown as percent of inventory.
Sheet stock and material spend for the job or period.
Cutting waste and offcut loss as percent of material cost.
Acceptable scrap target for similar jobs.
Bend, fit-up and finishing rework hours expected.
Powder coat, deburr and finishing spend tied to rework risk.
Rent, depreciation, admin and indirect burden for the month.
Billable or scheduled spindle hours per month.
Power, consumables and direct variable burden per hour.
